claim_text: Context7 exposes two MCP tools: resolve-library-id(libraryName, query) and query-docs(libraryId, query)
The 2026 API redesign removed client-side pagination, tokens budget, and topic/mode params; reranking is server-side
Claude Code plugin install does NOT auto-pass CONTEXT7_API_KEY from shell env; requires .mcp.json env injection or --api-key flag
Free tier (2026-05) is 1,000 calls/month with 20 bonus calls/day after cap
Free tier was changed Jan 2026: cut to 500 on 2026-01-13, raised to 1000 on 2026-01-16
The official Claude Code plugin bundles 5 surfaces: .mcp.json, plugin.json, docs-researcher agent, /context7:docs command, documentation-lookup skill
Recommended autonomous trigger pattern: 'Always use Context7 when I need library/API documentation, code generation, setup or configuration steps'
Context7 is the wrong tool for refactoring, business logic, scripts from scratch, debugging user code, general programming concepts
Context7 MCP has been documented to block Claude Code subagent spawn under specific transport conditions (Issue #877)
Schema validation can fail on resolve-library-id if both libraryName and query aren't passed (Issue #1706)
Common failure 'Documentation not found or not finalized for this version' indicates index gap; escalate to tavily_skill
MCP tool descriptions cost context tokens on every turn; broad auto-invoke wastes budget
Pro tier is $10/seat/month for 5,000 calls; overage is $10/1,000 calls
Hardcoding API key in versioned .mcp.json is a security antipattern; use ${ENV_VAR} interpolation
